<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./index.ts`

```text
     1→/**
     2→ * APOD Proxy — Cloudflare Worker
     3→ *
     4→ * Serves NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day from a stable URL.
     5→ * Uses Cloudflare's edge cache for both the API response and the
     6→ * image bytes — no KV, no cron, no external storage.
     7→ *
     8→ * Routes:
     9→ *   GET /     → today's APOD image (standard res)
    10→ *   GET /hd   → today's APOD image (high res)
    11→ *   GET /info → JSON metadata
    12→ */
    13→
    14→export interface Env {
    15→  NASA_API_KEY?: string;
    16→}
    17→
    18→const NASA_APOD_URL = "https://api.nasa.gov/planetary/apod";
    19→const MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS = 7;
    20→const CACHE_TTL = 3600;
    21→
    22→interface ApodResponse {
    23→  date: string;
    24→  title: string;
    25→  explanation: string;
    26→  url: string;
    27→  hdurl?: string;
    28→  media_type: "image" | "video";
    29→  copyright?: string;
    30→}
    31→
    32→function formatDate(d: Date): string {
    33→  return d.toISOString().slice(0, 10);
    34→}
    35→
    36→async function fetchApod(date: string, apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    37→  const url = `${NASA_APOD_URL}?api_key=${apiKey}&date=${date}`;
    38→
    39→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    40→    cf: { cacheTtl: CACHE_TTL, cacheEverything: true },
    41→  });
    42→
    43→  if (!res.ok) {
    44→    throw new Error(`NASA API ${res.status} for ${date}`);
    45→  }
    46→
    47→  return res.json() as Promise<ApodResponse>;
    48→}
    49→
    50→async function findLatestImage(apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    51→  const now = new Date();
    52→
    53→  for (let i = 0; i <= MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS; i++) {
    54→    const d = new Date(now);
    55→    d.setUTCDate(d.getUTCDate() - i);
    56→
    57→    try {
    58→      const apod = await fetchApod(formatDate(d), apiKey);
    59→      if (apod.media_type === "image") return apod;
    60→    } catch {
    61→      continue;
    62→    }
    63→  }
    64→
    65→  throw new Error("No image APOD found in the last week");
    66→}
    67→
    68→async function fetchImageCached(url: string): Promise<Response> {
    69→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    70→    cf: { cacheTtl: CACHE_TTL, cacheEverything: true },
    71→  });
    72→
    73→  if (!res.ok) {
    74→    throw new Error(`Image fetch failed: ${res.status}`);
    75→  }
    76→
    77→  return res;
    78→}
    79→
    80→function corsHeaders(headers: Record<string, string> = {}): Record<string, string> {
    81→  return {
    82→    "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
    83→    "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}`,
    84→    ...headers,
    85→  };
    86→}
    87→
    88→export default {
    89→  async fetch(request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {
    90→    if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {
    91→      return new Response(null, {
    92→        headers: {
    93→          "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
    94→          "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, OPTIONS",
    95→          "Access-Control-Max-Age": "86400",
    96→        },
    97→      });
    98→    }
    99→
   100→    const { pathname } = new URL(request.url);
   101→
   102→    try {
   103→      const apiKey = env.NASA_API_KEY || "DEMO_KEY";
   104→      const apod = await findLatestImage(apiKey);
   105→
   106→      if (pathname === "/" || pathname === "/hd") {
   107→        const imageUrl = pathname === "/hd"
   108→          ? (apod.hdurl || apod.url)
   109→          : apod.url;
   110→
   111→        const upstream = await fetchImageCached(imageUrl);
   112→        const contentType = upstream.headers.get("Content-Type") || "image/jpeg";
   113→
   114→        return new Response(upstream.body, {
   115→          headers: corsHeaders({ "Content-Type": contentType }),
   116→        });
   117→      }
   118→
   119→      if (pathname === "/info") {
   120→        return new Response(
   121→          JSON.stringify({
   122→            title: apod.title,
   123→            date: apod.date,
   124→            explanation: apod.explanation,
   125→            url: apod.url,
   126→            hdurl: apod.hdurl ?? null,
   127→            copyright: apod.copyright ?? null,
   128→          }, null, 2),
   129→          { headers: corsHeaders({ "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   130→        );
   131→      }
   132→
   133→      return new Response(
   134→        JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found. Routes: /, /hd, /info" }),
   135→        { status: 404, headers: corsHeaders({ "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   136→      );
   137→    } catch (err) {
   138→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
   139→      return new Response(
   140→        JSON.stringify({ error: message }),
   141→        { status: 502, headers: corsHeaders({ "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   142→      );
   143→    }
   144→  },
   145→};
   146→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./README.md`

```text
     1→# APOD Proxy
     2→
     3→A Cloudflare Worker that serves NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day from a **stable, never-changing URL**.
     4→
     5→Use it anywhere you need a fixed image URL that automatically updates daily — CSS backgrounds, digital signage, wallpaper feeds, etc.
     6→
     7→## Routes
     8→
     9→| Path | Response |
    10→|------|----------|
    11→| `/` | Today's APOD image (standard res) |
    12→| `/hd` | Today's APOD image (high res) |
    13→| `/info` | JSON metadata (title, date, explanation, copyright) |
    14→
    15→When the APOD is a video (happens occasionally), the worker walks backwards up to 7 days to find the most recent image.
    16→
    17→## How it works
    18→
    19→Both the NASA API call and the upstream image fetch use Cloudflare's [edge cache](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/runtime-apis/request/#requestinitcfproperties) via `cf: { cacheTtl, cacheEverything }`. First request per edge location per hour is a cache miss; everything after that is served directly from cache. No KV, no cron, no external storage.
    20→
    21→When the date rolls over, `formatDate(new Date())` produces a new date string, which changes the API request URL — a new cache key — so the response updates automatically.
    22→
    23→## Setup
    24→
    25→```bash
    26→npm install
    27→```
    28→
    29→### Local development
    30→
    31→```bash
    32→npx wrangler dev
    33→# Runs at http://localhost:8787
    34→```
    35→
    36→### Deploy
    37→
    38→```bash
    39→npx wrangler deploy
    40→```
    41→
    42→Your worker will be live at `https://apod-proxy.<your-subdomain>.workers.dev`.
    43→
    44→### NASA API key (optional)
    45→
    46→Without a key, the worker uses `DEMO_KEY` which is rate-limited to 30 requests/hour. Since the edge cache absorbs nearly all traffic, this is fine for most use cases. For higher limits (1,000 req/hr), get a free key at [api.nasa.gov](https://api.nasa.gov) and set it as a secret:
    47→
    48→```bash
    49→npx wrangler secret put NASA_API_KEY
    50→```
    51→
    52→### Custom domain (optional)
    53→
    54→To serve from your own domain, add a [`routes`](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/routing/routes/) entry to `wrangler.toml`:
    55→
    56→```toml
    57→routes = [
    58→  { pattern = "apod.yourdomain.com/*", zone_name = "yourdomain.com" }
    59→]
    60→```
    61→
    62→## Usage examples
    63→
    64→```css
    65→body {
    66→  background-image: url("https://apod-proxy.YOUR_SUBDOMAIN.workers.dev/hd");
    67→  background-size: cover;
    68→}
    69→```
    70→
    71→```html
    72→<img
    73→  src="https://apod-proxy.YOUR_SUBDOMAIN.workers.dev/"
    74→  alt="NASA Astronomy Picture of the Day"
    75→/>
    76→```
    77→
    78→```bash
    79→curl -s https://apod-proxy.YOUR_SUBDOMAIN.workers.dev/info | jq .
    80→```
    81→
    82→## Cost
    83→
    84→Free. The Cloudflare Workers free tier includes 100,000 requests/day. The NASA API key is also free.
    85→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 03:14:42Z)**_

Here's the analysis:

## APOD Proxy — Cloudflare Worker

A small, single-file Cloudflare Worker that proxies NASA's **Astronomy Picture of the Day** API behind a stable URL. Clean and focused — ~145 lines of TypeScript.

### What it does
- **`/`** — serves today's APOD image (standard res)
- **`/hd`** — serves the HD version
- **`/info`** — returns JSON metadata (title, date, explanation, copyright)
- Falls back up to 7 days if today's APOD is a video (not an image)

### Architecture
- **Runtime**: Cloudflare Workers (Wrangler v3)
- **Caching**: Leverages Cloudflare's edge cache via `cf: { cacheTtl, cacheEverything }` — both for the NASA API response and the upstream image bytes. 1-hour TTL. No KV, cron, or external storage needed.
- **API Key**: Uses `NASA_API_KEY` env secret, falls back to `DEMO_KEY` (rate-limited but fine behind cache).
- CORS enabled for all origins.

### Issues / Notes

1. **`wrangler.toml` says `main = "src/index.ts"` but the file is at `./index.ts`** — this will fail on deploy. Either move `index.ts` into a `src/` directory or update wrangler.toml to `main = "index.ts"`.

2. **`tsconfig.json` has `"include": ["src"]`** — same mismatch. The TS compiler won't pick up `index.ts` at the root.

3. **No `node_modules` / no lockfile** — `npm install` hasn't been run yet.

4. **Not a git repo** — no version control initialized.

5. **Every request calls `findLatestImage`** — even for `/info`, the full lookback loop runs. This is fine because Cloudflare's edge cache absorbs repeated API calls, but it means a cold-cache request to `/` could make up to 8 sequential fetches to NASA's API in the worst case.

6. **No input validation on pathname** — not a security concern (it just 404s), but paths like `/info/` (trailing slash) won't match.

Want me to fix the file path mismatch and get this project set up properly?

---

Please analyze this codebase and create a CLAUDE.md file, which will be given to future instances of Claude Code to operate in this repository.

What to add:
1. Commands that will be commonly used, such as how to build, lint, and run tests. Include the necessary commands to develop in this codebase, such as how to run a single test.
2. High-level code architecture and structure so that future instances can be productive more quickly. Focus on the "big picture" architecture that requires reading multiple files to understand.

Usage notes:
- If there's already a CLAUDE.md, suggest improvements to it.
- When you make the initial CLAUDE.md, do not repeat yourself and do not include obvious instructions like "Provide helpful error messages to users", "Write unit tests for all new utilities", "Never include sensitive information (API keys, tokens) in code or commits".
- Avoid listing every component or file structure that can be easily discovered.
- Don't include generic development practices.
- If there are Cursor rules (in .cursor/rules/ or .cursorrules) or Copilot rules (in .github/copilot-instructions.md), make sure to include the important parts.
- If there is a README.md, make sure to include the important parts.
- Do not make up information such as "Common Development Tasks", "Tips for Development", "Support and Documentation" unless this is expressly included in other files that you read.
- Be sure to prefix the file with the following text:

```
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
```

```md
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

APOD Proxy — a Cloudflare Worker that proxies NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day API behind stable, fixed URLs. Serves today's APOD image (standard or HD) or JSON metadata. Falls back up to 7 days when the daily APOD is a video.

## Commands

\```bash
npm run dev        # Local dev server at http://localhost:8787
npm run deploy     # Deploy to Cloudflare Workers
npx tsc --noEmit   # Type-check (no build artifact — Wrangler bundles directly)
\```

## Architecture

Single-file Worker (`src/index.ts`) with three routes:

- `GET /` — proxies today's standard-res APOD image
- `GET /hd` — proxies the HD version (falls back to standard if unavailable)
- `GET /info` — returns JSON metadata

Caching is handled entirely by Cloudflare's edge cache via `cf: { cacheTtl, cacheEverything }` on fetch calls — no KV, D1, or cron. Cache key changes naturally when the date rolls over (new date string = new API URL).

The `findLatestImage` function walks backwards from today up to `MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS` (7) to skip video APODs.

## Environment

- **`NASA_API_KEY`** — optional secret; falls back to `DEMO_KEY` (30 req/hr, sufficient behind cache). Set via `npx wrangler secret put NASA_API_KEY`.
- Local secrets go in `.dev.vars` (gitignored).

```

```text
File created successfully at: /Users/grant/code/nasapic/CLAUDE.md
```
</details></tool-use>
